scenes.name: hybrid_dynamics
scenes.worldSetup: World loaded via SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/fullbody1.skel"); gravity explicitly set to (0, -9.81, 0) (Y-up skeleton). Uses skeleton index 1 (the fullbody1 humanoid). Initial pose set through generalized-coordinate index list {1, 6(left hip), 9(left knee), 10(left ankle), 13(right hip), 16(right knee), 17(right ankle), 21(lower back)} = {-0.2, 0.15, -0.4, 0.25, 0.15, -0.4, 0.25, 0.0}. Joint 0 (root) set to Joint::PASSIVE; all remaining joints set to Joint::VELOCITY actuator type. Timestep from the skel file (not overridden). Window 640x480 (no gui-scale support in this example); camera home eye (5,3,3), center (0,0,0), up (0,1,0).
scenes.controllers: HybridDynamicsWorld : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreStep(): looks up joints by name on skeleton 1 and sets velocity commands each step: j_scapula_left = 1.0*sin(4*t), j_scapula_right = -1.0*sin(4*t), j_forearm_left = 0.8*sin(4*t), j_forearm_right = 0.8*sin(4*t), j_shin_left = 0.1*sin(2*t), j_shin_right = 0.1*sin(2*t) (t = world time). No customPostStep. Separate HybridDynamicsEventHandler (osgGA::GUIEventHandler) holds mHarnessOn bool; toggleHarness() switches the h_pelvis body's parent joint actuator type between Joint::LOCKED (harness on, prints "The pelvis is locked.") and Joint::PASSIVE (harness off, prints "The pelvis is unlocked.").
keyBindings: h / H: toggle harness on/off (lock/unlock pelvis parent joint LOCKED<->PASSIVE)
space: simulation on/off (Viewer built-in, advertised via addInstructionText)
tunables: arm sinusoid amplitude 1.0 / -1.0 (scapula) and 0.8 (forearms) at 4.0 rad/s (code constants, no GUI)
shin sinusoid amplitude 0.1 at 2.0 rad/s (code constant, no GUI)
scenes.interactions: Keyboard only via a custom osgGA::GUIEventHandler added with viewer.addEventHandler(). No DragAndDrop, no InteractiveFrame, no ImGui, no mouse picking.
scenes.portRisks: Hardcoded getSkeleton(1) index and joint/body names (h_pelvis, j_scapula_*, j_forearm_*, j_shin_*) must match fullbody1.skel. Harness state lives in the event handler, not the WorldNode, so the port must route the host's key events to the same toggle and keep state consistent (e.g. on scene reset the pelvis joint actuator type must be restored to PASSIVE). Runtime switching of actuator types (LOCKED/PASSIVE/VELOCITY) is the core of the demo — a host that rebuilds the skeleton per activation must reapply the actuator setup. This is the only one of the six that does not parse --gui-scale. Y-up world: camera/grid in a shared host must handle up-axis (0,1,0) for this scene.

scenes.name: biped_stand
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/fullbody1.skel"); gravity (0, -9.81, 0) (Y-up). Skeleton fetched by name "fullbody1". Initial DOF poses set by name: j_pelvis_rot_y=-0.20, j_thigh_left_z=0.15, j_shin_left=-0.40, j_heel_left_1=0.25, j_thigh_right_z=0.15, j_shin_right=-0.40, j_heel_right_1=0.25, j_abdomen_2=0.00. Default shadow technique created via WorldNode::createDefaultShadowTechnique(&viewer) and passed into the node. Simulation starts paused (space to start). Window 640x480 scaled by parseGuiScaleOptions (--gui-scale / -h help). Camera home eye (3,1.5,3), center (0,0,0), up (0,1,0).
scenes.controllers: CustomWorldNode : RealTimeWorldNode. Ctor caches h_heel_left BodyNode, DOF indices of j_heel_left_1/j_toe_left and j_heel_right_1/j_toe_right, timestep, Kp/Kd diagonal matrices (rows 0-5 zero; Kp=400, Kd=40 for actuated dofs), desired dofs = initial positions, zero torque/force state. customPreStep(): (1) perturbation — adds mForce as external force on h_spine while mImpulseDuration>0, decrementing each step; (2) SPD tracking: invM=(M+Kd*dt)^-1, p=-Kp*(q+dq*dt-qDesired), d=-Kd*dq, qddot=invM*(-Cg+p+d+constraintForces), torques=p+d-Kd*qddot*dt; (3) ankle strategy in sagittal plane from offset = COM.x - COP.x (COP = leftHeel transform * (0.05,0,0)): if 0<offset<0.1 gains k1=200,k2=100,kd=10; if -0.2<offset<-0.05 gains k1=2000,k2=100,kd=100; adds -k1*offset+kd*(prevOffset-offset) to heel dofs and -k2 variant to toe dofs on both feet, tracking mPreOffset; (4) zeros the 6 root torques and calls setForces. perturbBiped(force, frames=100) arms the impulse. customPreRefresh/customPostRefresh/customPostStep are empty stubs. CustomEventHandler holds a raw pointer to the node and maps keys 1-4 to perturbBiped.
keyBindings: 1: push robot +50 N along x-axis for 100 frames
2: push robot -50 N along x-axis for 100 frames
3: push robot +50 N along z-axis for 100 frames
4: push robot -50 N along z-axis for 100 frames
space: start/pause simulation (Viewer built-in, advertised via addInstructionText)
tunables: SPD gains Kp=400, Kd=40 per actuated dof (code constants)
ankle strategy gain sets (k1=200/2000, k2=100, kd=10/100) and offset windows (0,0.1) / (-0.2,-0.05) (code constants)
perturbation magnitude 50 N and duration 100 frames (code constants, safe ~10-200 N)
scenes.interactions: Keyboard only (custom GUIEventHandler with raw node pointer). No DnD or ImGui. Uses default shadow technique for rendering.
scenes.portRisks: Dense (M+Kd*dt).inverse() every physics step (nDof~37) — fine standalone but worth budgeting in a consolidated host stepping multiple scenes. Perturbation state (mForce/mImpulseDuration) and mPreOffset live on the WorldNode and must reset with the scene. Desired pose captured at construction time from the initial positions — if the host re-poses the skeleton after node construction the controller will track the wrong target. Event handler keys 1-4 collide with operational_space_control's held-key axis constraints if both scenes share one key router. Y-up scene.

scenes.name: operational_space_control
scenes.worldSetup: Fresh empty World (gravity left at World default (0,0,-9.81)). DartLoader parses robot "dart://sample/urdf/KR5/KR5 sixx R650.urdf" and ground "dart://sample/urdf/KR5/ground.urdf". Robot root joint transform-from-parent reset to Identity so Z is up; ground pre-translated (0,0,0.5) and rotated pi/2 about X. In the node ctor: end effector = last BodyNode; every joint gets setLimitEnforcement(false) and setDampingCoefficient(0, 0.5); Kp = 50*I3, Kd = 5*I(nDof); target = SimpleFrame "target" with red (0.9,0,0) SphereShape r=0.025 placed at EE world tf pretranslated by offset (0.05,0,0), added to world; mOffset then rotated into EE body frame. node->setNumStepsPerCycle(10); viewer.simulate(true) so it runs immediately. Window 640x480 scaled (--gui-scale). Camera home eye (2.57,3.14,1.64), center (0,0,0), up (-0.24,-0.25,0.94) (Z-up-ish).
scenes.controllers: OperationalSpaceControlWorld : WorldNode (plain, not RealTime; 10 sim steps per render cycle). customPreStep(): computes M, damped pseudoinverses of the EE linear Jacobian J and its derivative dJ at mOffset (damping term 0.0025*I3), position error e = target translation - EE*offset, velocity error de = -EE linear velocity at offset, Cg, then forces = M*(pinv_J*Kp*de + pinv_dJ*Kp*e) + Cg + Kd*pinv_J*Kp*e; setForces on robot. setupViewer() override (runs when node is added to viewer): enables DragAndDrop on the target frame, dnd->setObstructable(false), and adds instruction text for dragging plus keys 1/2/3. ConstraintEventHandler tracks held-key state in mConstrained[3] and maps it to dnd->constrainToLine (1 axis), constrainToPlane (2 axes), or unconstrain (0 or 3 axes); holds the DnD via dart::sub_ptr. ShadowEventHandler toggles the node's shadow technique.
keyBindings: 1 (hold; KEYDOWN sets, KEYUP clears): constrain target dragging to the x-axis
2 (hold): constrain target dragging to the y-axis
3 (hold): constrain target dragging to the z-axis (combinations: one held key -> line, two held keys -> plane through the two axes, zero or all three -> unconstrained)
s / S: toggle shadows on/off (creates/clears default shadow technique)
space: pause/resume simulation (Viewer built-in; starts running because viewer.simulate(true))
tunables: joint damping coefficient 0.5, joint limit enforcement disabled (code constants)
scenes.interactions: DragAndDrop on the red target SimpleFrame (unobstructable so it can be grabbed through the robot), with held-key axis/plane constraints; shadow toggle key. Target dragging is the primary interaction — controller chases the ball continuously.
scenes.portRisks: public 'dnd' member is only initialized inside setupViewer(), i.e. after viewer.addWorldNode(node) — a port that constructs handlers before attaching the node passes a garbage/dangling pointer (main.cpp relies on addWorldNode running setupViewer first; ConstraintEventHandler tolerates null but not uninitialized). DnD registration is per-Viewer, so a consolidated host must re-enable it on scene activation and disable on switch. Keys 1/2/3 use both KEYDOWN and KEYUP (held-key semantics) — a key router that only forwards presses breaks the constraint feature; also collides with biped_stand's 1-4 bindings. Plain WorldNode + numStepsPerCycle=10 means sim speed is render-rate dependent, unlike the RealTimeWorldNode examples. Gravity is the implicit World default (Z-up), not set explicitly.

scenes.name: contact_inverse_dynamics
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/fullbody1.skel") with null-check; gravity (0,-9.81,0) (Y-up). Skeleton "fullbody1" fetched by name; same initial DOF pose as biped_stand set via a warning-emitting helper (j_pelvis_rot_y=-0.20, j_thigh_left_z=0.15, j_shin_left=-0.40, j_heel_left_1=0.25, j_thigh_right_z=0.15, j_shin_right=-0.40, j_heel_right_1=0.25, j_abdomen_2=0.00). Node ctor: ContactInverseDynamics solver on the biped with setRegularization(1e-4) and setResidualTolerance(1e-4); caches h_heel_left/h_toe_left/h_heel_right/h_toe_right (invalid -> isValid()=false, exit 1); creates 4 SimpleFrames each holding a green ArrowShape (radius 0.01, head props 2.0/0.15) added to the world; findTorqueDofs picks the first 3 existing of {j_thigh_left_z, j_shin_left, j_heel_left_1, j_thigh_right_z, j_shin_right, j_heel_right_1}; stores initial pose; generateSquatKeyframes(0.15): 60 keyframes via hierarchical whole-body IK — heel IKs (level 0) pinned to initial heel poses, pelvis (bodynode 0) IK (level 1) target lowered by depth*0.5*(1-cos(2*pi*phase)); GradientDescentSolver max iterations 200 for the first solve then 50. ImGuiViewer, window 1280x720 scaled (guiScale from DART_GUI_SCALE env and/or --gui-scale flag, both '--gui-scale X' and '--gui-scale=X'); default shadow technique in interactive mode; camera eye (3,1.5,3), center origin, up (0,1,0).
scenes.controllers: ContactInverseDynamicsWorldNode : WorldNode — the world is never physically stepped; everything happens in customPreRefresh(): (1) consumes std::atomic<bool> mRegenerateRequested (set by widget callback) and regenerates keyframes at the widget's squat depth; (2) if widget Play is on, advances mTime += (1/60)*playbackSpeed; phase = fmod(time, 3.0s)/3.0; (3) closed Catmull-Rom spline interpolation over the 60 keyframes gives q, and analytic spline derivatives give dq (scaled by phaseRate = speed/cycleDuration; 0 when paused so paused = gravity compensation) and ddq (scaled by phaseRate^2); sets positions/velocities/accelerations kinematically; (4) builds 4 ContactInverseDynamics::Contact entries (bodies = LHeel,LToe,RHeel,RToe; localOffset zero; normal = -normalized gravity; frictionCoeff from widget; numBasis 8), setContacts + compute(); (5) pushes feasibility, infinity-norm unactuated residual, contact forces, joint forces, torque names/dofs into the widget; (6) updates arrow frames: tail at body world position, head at tail + forceScale*force, hidden when |force|<1e-8, color green when feasible / red when infeasible via notifyColorUpdated. Widget ContactInverseDynamicsWidget : ImGuiWidget renders the panel and owns play/speed/depth/friction/scale state plus 240-sample ring buffers for GRF and 3 joint-torque plots; regenerate is marshalled through node->requestKeyframeRegeneration() to avoid touching the skeleton from the draw thread.
keyBindings: (none custom — all control is through the ImGui panel; ImGuiViewer built-in space would toggle world stepping but the demo is purely kinematic and starts paused)
tunables: Squat Depth (ImGui slider, 0.0-0.25 m; keyframes regenerate on slider release via IsItemDeactivatedAfterEdit)
Menu > Exit; Help > About DART (ImGui menu bar)
--frames N (frame-limited run) and --screenshot PATH (capture last frame; writes PATH_0_<frame>.png) CLI
scenes.interactions: ImGui panel only (control sliders, status readout FEASIBLE/INFEASIBLE, residual norm, per-contact force magnitudes, vertical-force/body-weight ratio, PlotLines for 3 joint torques [-200,200] and total vertical GRF [0,1000]). No DnD or custom key handler. Frame-limited mode attaches an osgViewer::ScreenCaptureHandler.
scenes.portRisks: Requires DART 6.19+ (ContactInverseDynamics). Draw-thread discipline: the ImGui regenerate callback must not mutate the skeleton — it only sets an atomic flag consumed in customPreRefresh; a port must preserve this deferral. Motion is driven kinematically in customPreRefresh, so if the host ever enables world stepping (space in ImGuiViewer) physics would fight the kinematic poses — the port should disable simulation for this scene. Widget<->node coupling: node holds shared_ptr to widget while the regenerate lambda captures a raw node pointer (lifetime must outlive the widget). generateSquatKeyframes creates persistent IK modules (getIK(true)) and SimpleFrame targets on the shared skeleton — reactivating the scene should not stack duplicates. Fixed 1/60 time increment assumes ~60 FPS refresh; plot ranges and 240-sample buffers are hardcoded. main() has two near-duplicate viewer setup paths (frame-limited without shadows vs interactive with shadows) to preserve.

scenes.name: ssik_ik_gui
scenes.worldSetup: Empty World, never stepped (pure kinematics), Z-up presentation (GridVisual on XY plane, camera up (0,0,1), eye (2.6,-3.0,1.6), center (0.1,0,0.55)). A 6-DOF draggable InteractiveFrame gizmo "ssik_target" (size 0.25) at default target (0.45, 0, 0.35) plus a non-interactive cyan SimpleFrame sphere r=0.05 "ssik_fk_solution" (offset +0.25 in y initially) are added as SimpleFrames. Arm geometry is built entirely in code by ArmSkeleton from the ssik module's product-of-exponentials chain: WeldJoint base then Revolute/Prismatic joints with mT_ParentBodyToJoint=t_left, mT_ChildBodyToJoint=t_right^-1, sphere joints (r=0.045) and cylinder links (r=0.028); one skeleton instance per IK solution (capped at 16), selected solution opaque red (0.90,0.20,0.18,1.0), ghosts translucent (0.85,0.32,0.30,0.22), rest-pose grey when no solutions. Viewer: ImGuiViewer forced to SingleThreaded threading model (GIL), window 1180x860 scaled. PythonSession RAII owns Py_Initialize/Py_Finalize.
scenes.controllers: SsikWorldNode is a plain WorldNode with no overrides — all logic lives in SsikIkWidget (ImGuiWidget) and the embedded Python bridge. Widget render(): detects 3D-gizmo drags by comparing mTarget->getTransform() against mAppliedTargetTf (1e-9 isApprox) and, if Auto solve is on, re-solves and syncs the panel fields; renders arm selector (19 ssik.prebuilt modules), target controls, solver controls, solution browser, all with hover tooltips. SsikPythonBridge: runs an in-source Python bridge script (importlib load of ssik.prebuilt.<module>, exposes BASE_LINK/EE_LINK/DOF/T_HOME/CHAIN and solve() with max_solutions/q_seed/respect_limits/allow_refinement/seed_metric, returning q, fk_residual, refinement_used, fk_pose per solution); every C API block is wrapped in a GilGuard; errors surfaced via consumePythonError into the status line. loadSelectedArm() rebuilds the ArmSkeleton pool, resets the target to the arm's home pose, and solves; graceful degraded mode with install hint when ssik import fails. selectArmForTest() is the --self-test hook (exit 1 if any arm fails to load or zero total solutions).
keyBindings: (none custom — no keyboard handler; interaction is mouse-drag of the gizmo plus the ImGui panel, and standard Viewer built-ins)
tunables: Arm (ImGui combo, 19 prebuilt arms: UR5, Puma 560, JACO 2, iiwa14, Gen3, Panda, xArm7, xArm6, Z1, PiPER, Rizon 4, Kassow KR810, Rizon 10, FANUC CRX-10iA/L, YAM, big_yam, FR3, OpenArm left/right; switching rebuilds + re-solves)
Position (ImGui DragFloat3, 0.005 m/step, meters in base frame)
Home target (ImGui button: reset target to arm home pose)
Read dragged (ImGui button: copy gizmo pose into fields)
Use q seed (ImGui checkbox, default off)
Seed from selection (ImGui button: copy selected solution q into seed, enable Use q seed)
Solution (ImGui SliderInt 0..n-1, browses branches; selected opaque, others ghosted)
Menu > Exit; Help > About DART (ImGui menu bar)
CLI: --arm INDEX (initial arm), --headless (one pbuffer frame to PNG then exit), --shot PATH (default ssik_ik_gui.png), --self-test (load+solve every arm, exit code), --gui-scale / -h
scenes.interactions: viewer->enableDragAndDrop on the InteractiveFrame gizmo (translation arrows, rotation rings, planar handles); bidirectional sync between gizmo drags and panel fields (render() drag detection + syncTargetFrame writeback); ImGui panel with per-widget tooltips; solved-pose cyan marker follows the selected solution's FK pose.
assets: (no dart:// assets — arm kinematics come from the optional Python package ssik, modules ssik.prebuilt.<name>; all render geometry is generated in code)
scenes.portRisks: Heaviest port: embeds CPython. Python.h must be included before all other headers; PythonSession must own exactly one interpreter (a host embedding Python elsewhere, or multiple scene activations, must not double-Init/Finalize); every solver call needs the GIL (GilGuard), and the viewer is forced SingleThreaded because an OSG draw thread calling into Python while the main thread holds the GIL deadlocks — a consolidated demos host with a multithreaded viewer will freeze on first solve unless the same constraint is applied. ssik is an optional pip dependency with graceful degradation (status message + install hint) that must be preserved. CMake: needs Python3::Python (Interpreter+Development) and skips the example when absent — the demos host must make this scene conditionally compiled. ArmSkeleton adds/removes up to 16 skeletons from the world at runtime (scene switch must clear the pool). --headless pbuffer path and --self-test exit-code contract (0 only on all-arms-loaded and >0 solutions) should carry over per the repo's example exit-code conventions.

scenes.name: wam_ikfast
scenes.worldSetup: Empty World; viewer.allowSimulation(false) — purely kinematic (help text says so explicitly). WAM arm created in Helpers.cpp: DartLoader with addPackageDirectory("herb_description", DART_DATA_PATH"/urdf/wam") parsing DART_DATA_PATH"/urdf/wam/wam.urdf"; startup configuration sets dofs "/j1".."/j7" to 0. Ground built in code: WeldJoint + 10x10x0.01 BoxShape at z=-0.005 with Visual/Collision/Dynamics aspects, color Color::Blue(0.2). setupEndEffectors: EndEffector "ee" on body "/wam7" with default relative transform translated (0,0,-0.09); IK target = InteractiveFrame "lh_target" at World; gradient method set to SharedLibraryIkFast loading "<DART_SHARED_LIB_PREFIX>wamIk[d].<DART_SHARED_LIB_EXTENSION>" ('d' suffix on Linux/macOS debug builds) with ikFastDofs {0,1,3,4,5,6} and free dof {2}; error-method linear and angular bounds set to +/-infinity. Window 1280x960 scaled (--gui-scale). Camera eye (5.34,3.00,1.91), center (0,0,0.5), up (0,0,0.98) — Z-up.
scenes.controllers: WamWorld : WorldNode with only customPreRefresh(): mWam->getIK(true)->solveAndApply(true) every rendered frame, so the whole-skeleton hierarchical IK continuously chases whatever targets are active/dragged. InputHandler (osgGA::GUIEventHandler) captures at construction: mRestConfig = current positions, and per EndEffector-with-IK: default error bounds, default target relative transform, a constraint-active flag, and the EE index; keys then toggle those constraints (activate: reset bounds to standard defaults, snap target to current EE transform, add target frame to world; deactivate: restore stored custom bounds and default target tf, remove frame from world).
keyBindings: p / P: print every DOF name and current position to stdout
t / T: reset the robot to its rest (startup) configuration

1-9: toggle the interactive IK target of end effector #N (only index 1 exists for the single "ee"; toggling adds/removes the lh_target frame from the world and swaps IK error bounds between standard defaults and the example's infinite bounds)
Alt + Click (drag): translate a body without changing its orientation (BodyNode DnD built-in, advertised in help text)
Ctrl + Click (drag): rotate a body without changing its translation (BodyNode DnD built-in)
Shift + Click (drag): move a body using only its parent joint (BodyNode DnD built-in)
tunables: rest configuration (captured at startup; key t restores)
IK error bounds: infinite custom bounds vs standard defaults, swapped by target toggle (code-driven, no GUI)
--gui-scale <0.75-4.0> CLI window/UI scale (no ImGui panel in this example)
scenes.interactions: DragAndDrop enabled on every WAM BodyNode (viewer.enableDragAndDrop(bodyNode, false, false)) so the arm can be posed by dragging links with Alt/Ctrl/Shift modifiers, and on the IK target InteractiveFrame when its EE constraint is active (enableDragAndDrops scans EEs whose IK target is an InteractiveFrame). customPreRefresh IK makes the arm follow the dragged gizmo live. Simulation is disallowed entirely.
assets: DART_DATA_PATH/urdf/wam/wam.urdf (plus wam mesh files resolved via package name herb_description -> DART_DATA_PATH/urdf/wam)
examples/wam_ikfast/ikfast/ikfast71.Transform6D.4_6_9_10_11_12_f8.cpp (compiled into the separate shared library wamIk that SharedLibraryIkFast dlopens at runtime)
scenes.portRisks: SharedLibraryIkFast loads libwamIk by filename at runtime: the demos host must keep building the ikfast subdirectory target (SHARED lib, IKFAST_NO_MAIN + IKFAST_CLIBRARY defines, -w, CMAKE_DEBUG_POSTFIX "d", output dir DART_BINARY_DIR) and guarantee the library name (DART_SHARED_LIB_PREFIX/EXTENSION + debug 'd' suffix logic in Helpers.cpp) resolves from the host binary's library search path — a silent load failure degrades IK to no-op. Uses the DART_DATA_PATH compile definition rather than dart:// URIs. viewer.allowSimulation(false) is a per-Viewer setting a shared host must emulate per-scene. Body DnD is enabled on every BodyNode against the specific Viewer and must be torn down/re-registered on scene switch. InputHandler snapshots rest config and default bounds at construction — scene re-entry must rebuild it or state (active targets, world-added frames) leaks. Keys 1-9/t/p may collide with other scenes' bindings in a consolidated key router.
